title: Fresh Fluffy Korean Egg Broth
description: A light, airy egg broth that soothes sick days - sweet-free, pressure-cooked, and packed with gentle flavors.
instructions: In a pressure‑cooker, combine water, chicken broth (if using), salt, pepper, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and seaweed. Stir to dissolve salt.
Seal the cooker and set to high pressure for 5 minutes. This infuses the broth with gentle flavors.
Quickly release pressure, open the lid, and add the cubed tofu and chopped bok choy. Close the lid and bring back to high pressure, cooking for 2 minutes.
While the vegetables are cooking, gently whisk the eggs in a bowl until the yolks and whites are fully blended.
After the 2-minute cooking, release pressure again and carefully pour the whisked eggs into the hot broth in a slow, steady stream, swirling to form thin ribbons.
Restore the lid, set to low pressure for 1 minute to finish cooking the eggs without curdling.
Quickly release pressure, open the lid, and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if needed.
Serve the broth hot, garnished with fresh green onion slices and a drizzle of sesame oil.
If desired, add a splash of soy sauce for extra umami, keeping it low in sodium.
Enjoy the comforting, fluffy broth within 15 minutes of serving.
